response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically strong individual contributor with broad full-stack experience and genuine exposure to developer tooling (CI/CD setup, test frameworks, MCP tooling), but the role is fundamentally a people-management position requiring demonstrated experience leading engineering teams, conducting performance reviews, and driving organizational change. There is no evidence in the resume of managing direct reports, owning headcount, or building engineering culture — the closest signals are community workshops and a Staff title, neither of which constitutes people leadership. The current caregiving career gap and absence of any management tenure make this a poor fit for a Level II Engineering Manager role.
strengths: 20+ years of software development experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure, well exceeding the 8-year minimum
Demonstrated developer tooling work: built open-source test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), configured CI/CD pipelines and GitLab infrastructure, and contributed to MCP code generators at re:cinq
Strong knowledge-sharing culture evidenced by organizing Refactoring Dojos, Code Kata sessions, and creating full-day public workshops on testing and web internals
concerns: No identifiable people management experience — no direct reports, no performance management, no hiring or org-building mentioned anywhere in the resume
Developer tooling work is incidental across roles rather than a sustained, primary focus; does not meet the 3-year dedicated developer tools requirement
Currently in an open-ended career gap (caregiving since October 2025) with no signal of readiness to step into a high-accountability leadership role
